ocr.text: The severity of Helicobacter pylori colonization is categorized as Mild, Moderate, and Severe, with a chi-square P value for
histopathological findings of 0.001 for metaplasia, where 30 (8.60%) showed Yes and 319 (91.40%) showed No in Mild, 32
(19.63%) Yes and 131 (80.37%) No in Moderate, and 6 (18.75%) Yes and 26 (81.25%) No in Severe; for atrophy, the P value is
0.004, with 13 (3.72%) Yes and 336 (96.28%) No in Mild, 15 (9.20%) Yes and 148 (90.80%) No in Moderate, and 5 (15.63%)
Yes and 27 (84.38%) No in Severe; for ulcer, the P value is 0.07*, with 10 (2.87%) Yes and 339 (97.13%) No in Mild, 10
(6.13%) Yes and 153 (93.87%) No in Moderate, and 3 (9.38%) Yes and 29 (90.63%) No in Severe; for lymphoid follicle, the P
value is 0.001, with 106 (34.53%) Yes and 201 (65.47%) No in Mild, 100 (61.73%) Yes and 62 (38.27%) No in Moderate, and
25 (78.13%) Yes and 7 (21.88%) No in Severe. Questions regarding dietary changes since coming to Canada include general
portion size with answer possibilities on a 5-point Likert scale: 1. Eat much less, 2.3. No change, and 4.5. Eat much more;
methods of food preparation inquiry including - Stir-frying/BBQ, Baking/grilling food, Boiling food, Deep frying food,
Microwaving food, and Eating vegetables raw, with the same answer possibilities; changes in food types such as - Vegetables,
Potatoes, rice, Convenience foods, Fruit, Soft drinks/pop/soda, Dairy products, High fat/fried foods, Deserts/candy/sweets,
White meat, Red meat, and Restaurant meals/dining out; interest in food information including - I look at ingredients in the
food I buy, I put effort into making sure the food I buy has good nutritional value, I read the nutritional information table on
food products, I understand the nutritional information table on food products, and I hear about which foods are good for me
through the media and advertising, with answer possibilities of 1. Much less often, 2.3.
The severity of Helicobacter pylori colonization  Mild  Moderate  Severe  P (chi-square)
Histopathological findings  Metaplasia  Yes  30 (8.60%)  32 (19.63%)  6 (18.75%)  0.001
Questions  Sub-items  Answer possibilities(5-point Likert scale)
Has your general portion size (how much you eat in one meal) changed since coming to Canada?  - No sub-items  1. Eat much less2.3. No change4.5. Eat much more

Much less often2.3. No change4.5. Much more often

How do you feel about these issues in Canada compared to your home country?  - Finding fresh fruit and vegetables- Finding low fat food options- Choosing healthy food when dining out  1. Much harder in Canada2.3. No Change4.5. Much easier in Canada
No change, and 4.5. Much more often; and feelings about food issues in Canada compared to home country regarding - Finding
fresh fruit and vegetables, Finding low fat food options, Choosing healthy food when dining out, with answer possibilities of 1.
Much harder in Canada, 2.3. No Change, and 4.5. Much easier in Canada.
